# Bootstrap CI Report (generated by eval/bootstrap_ci.py)

Paired bootstrap over the 160 `eval/dev_labels.jsonl` candidates, B=5000, seed=1234.

**Baseline dev composite = 0.8682**, 95% CI [0.7633, 0.9599] (width 0.1966).

| Variant | point delta | 95% CI | P(delta<=0) | zero in CI? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| C1 collaborative-filtering->concept | -0.0006 | [-0.0396, +0.0007] | 0.870 | yes |
| C2 skill_assessment positive | -0.0001 | [-0.0004, +0.0001] | 0.697 | yes |

**Reading this:** a 160-candidate dev set carries real sampling noise — the baseline composite's true value could plausibly be anywhere in the CI above. Where zero falls inside a variant's CI, that delta is statistically indistinguishable from noise; "REJECT" for such a variant means "not proven to help, and kept out on the qualitative JD-alignment argument recorded in eval/impact.py," not "proven harmful."
